skills.name: Apply for a permit, license, or benefit
skills.desc: Start and track an application to the city — a building permit, business or professional license, benefit (SNAP/cash/Medicaid), grant, school or college admission, or lottery.
skills.whenToUse: The resident wants to apply for, enroll in, or file for something the city grants. Resolve the right agency, collect the required fields against its schema, submit, and return a tracking id.

skills.name: Report a problem or file a complaint
skills.desc: File a 311-style service request or a complaint/tip with the agency that owns it — noise, a pothole, a building condition, a consumer or rights complaint, a tip to an investigator.
skills.whenToUse: The resident is reporting an issue or misconduct. Classify the problem, route it to the owning agency's report tool, attach a location (BBL/BIN/coordinates), and return a service-request id.

skills.name: Request public records
skills.desc: Make a records request — a FOIL request, a vital record (birth/death/marriage), a certified document, or a transcript.
skills.whenToUse: The resident needs an official copy or a records disclosure. Identify the record type and custodian agency, submit the request, and track fulfillment.

skills.name: Schedule or reserve
skills.desc: Book an appointment, inspection, hearing slot, facility, or hold — an OATH hearing, a DOB inspection, a park/venue reservation, a library hold.
skills.whenToUse: The resident needs a time or a resource reserved. Find availability, reserve it, and return a confirmation.

skills.name: Register or enroll
skills.desc: Register or enroll with the city — register to vote or request a ballot, register a business or vendor, enroll in a program.
skills.whenToUse: The resident is establishing a standing status. Collect the required identity/eligibility fields, register, and confirm.

skills.name: Dispute or appeal
skills.desc: Challenge a city determination — appeal a property-tax assessment, dispute a violation or ticket, respond to a summons, file a grievance.
skills.whenToUse: The resident is contesting a charge or decision. Identify the case, assemble the response against the agency's rules, and file the dispute/appeal.

skills.name: Pay a city charge
skills.desc: Pay a fine, fee, tax, or bill owed to the city and get a receipt.
skills.whenToUse: The resident owes the city money. Resolve the charge, take payment, and return a confirmation.

skills.name: Look up a property or place
skills.desc: Resolve a NYC address, BBL, or BIN and return everything the city knows about that place across agencies — its districts, who represents it, and its records (buildings, housing, environment, complaints).
skills.whenToUse: The question is anchored to a location ("what's near me?", "who represents this block?", "what's on file for this building?"). Resolve to a nyc-commons Place and fan out across the agencies keyed on its BBL/BIN.

skills.name: Check application or case status
skills.desc: Look up the status of an application, permit, complaint, case, or request across agencies.
skills.whenToUse: The resident wants to know where something stands. Identify the case by its id/owner and return its current status and history.

skills.name: Search city data and records
skills.desc: Search and read the city's open records and datasets — filings, permits, violations, licenses, budgets, contracts, results — the read surface across all 67 agencies.
skills.whenToUse: The question needs facts from a city dataset or record. Pick the owning agency's search/get tools and return the records.
